command: $baseline=(Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing 'https://assets.example.local/baselines/common-domains.txt').Content -split "`n"; $recent=Get-WinEvent -LogName 'Microsoft-Windows-DNS-Client/Operational' -MaxEvents 500 | ForEach-Object { $_.Properties[2].Value } | Where-Object { $_ }; $rare=$recent | Where-Object { $baseline -notcontains $_ } | Group-Object | Where-Object { $_.Count -le 2 } | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Name; Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing -Method POST -ContentType 'application/json' -Body (@{ts=(Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString('o'); rare=$rare}|ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3) 'https://siem.example.local/api/ingest/rare-domains'
explanation: Defensive rarity analysis for T1568: highlights rarely seen domains from DNS client logs that may be tied to DGA/dynamic resolution and sends to SIEM.
rationale_steps: Collect recent queried domains from DNS client log.
Identify rare domains not in baseline with low frequency.
POST findings to SIEM for investigation.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Balanced quoting and parentheses with valid PowerShell operators and parameters; mixed named/positional arguments for Invoke-WebRequest are acceptable.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It gathers DNS client queries, filters against a baseline, surfaces rare domains, and posts to a SIEM as claimed, though use of a specific event field index and simple newline splitting (CRLF) plus potential log read permissions may cause minor inaccuracies.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is well-formed PowerShell with balanced parentheses, proper semicolons as statement separators, valid backtick escaping for the newline split, correct hashtable and ConvertTo-Json usage, and properly quoted strings throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements a defensive T1568 detection workflow: it fetches a known-good domain baseline, extracts queried domains from the DNS Client operational event log, identifies rare/low-frequency domains not in the baseline (a strong DGA/dynamic resolution signal), and ships the findings as structured JSON to a SIEM endpoint — all operationally plausible and well-aligned with the described rarity-analysis intent.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is perfectly well-formed, using correct syntax for variable assignment, piping, property access, and web requests.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements a rarity analysis workflow by baselining common domains, extracting recent DNS queries from event logs, and identifying outliers to detect potential DGA activity.
